core_mechanism: reduction of a nonlinear dispersive wave equation to effective finite-dimensional particle dynamics for its shape-preserving localized coherent structures (solitons), which collide quasi-elastically and whose centers of mass obey an effective equation of motion set by an external potential, with small corrections from radiative energy loss
brief_reason: The abstract shows the solitons keep their L2 shape through a fast collision while the dynamics of their centers of mass is approximately governed by the external potential plus error terms from radiation damping, i.e. the infinite-dimensional PDE is reduced to effective particle motion of coherent structures.
